translation.en: She is very presentable in the performance.

content.sentence: Cô ấy rất ___ trong buổi trình diễn.

notes_md:
**diện** usually means *to dress up / wear something stylish*. It’s about putting effort into how you look, not just wearing clothes.

It’s a **verb**, often used with clothes or outfits: you “diện” something a bit special or eye-catching.  

It often implies looking polished or fashionable, not everyday casual wear.  
Close idea: *ăn diện* (very common) = to dress up nicely.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I am getting my appearance ready to go to the party tonight.
He always pays attention to his appearance when going to work.
She likes her appearance in new clothes during Tet holidays.

content.sentence: Cô ấy rất ___ trong buổi trình diễn.

translation.en: She is very presentable in the performance.

exampleSentences: Em chuẩn bị diện đẹp để đi dự tiệc tối nay.
Anh ấy luôn chú ý đến diện mỗi khi đi làm.
Chị thích diện quần áo mới vào dịp lễ Tết.
notes:
diện usually means to dress up / wear something stylish. It’s about putting effort into how you look, not just wearing clothes.

It’s a verb, often used with clothes or outfits: you “diện” something a bit special or eye-catching.  

It often implies looking polished or fashionable, not everyday casual wear.  
Close idea: ăn diện (very common) = to dress up nicely.

example.vi: Cô ấy có một diện mạo xinh đẹp.

definitions.es: El aspecto exterior o la apariencia de alguien o algo.
definitions.en: The outward aspect or appearance of someone or something.
definitions.vi: Diện mạo hoặc vẻ bề ngoài của ai đó hoặc một cái gì đó.
